question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the gRPC `getNodes()` endpoint with unlimited concurrent requests (no rate limiting or connection cap enforced at the controller level), causing thread pool exhaustion on `Schedulers.boundedElastic()` and crashing the mirror node gRPC service so that no client can discover network nodes to submit transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send an `AddressBookQuery` with `limit=0` (the default), causing `networkService::getNodes` to use `Long.MAX_VALUE` in the `.take()` operator and stream every address book entry indefinitely, holding a gRPC stream open and exhausting server-side reactive scheduler threads, leading to total service unavailability?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of simultaneous `getNodes()` streams with `limit=0`, each holding a `Schedulers.boundedElastic()` thread via the `repeatWhen` loop in `NetworkServiceImpl`, exhausting the bounded elastic thread pool and causing all subsequent gRPC calls (including address book queries needed for transaction submission) to queue indefinitely or be rejected?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send an `AddressBookQuery` with `limit=Integer.MAX_VALUE` (2147483647), which is passed directly via `query.getLimit()` into `AddressBookFilter.limit` and then used in `.take(filter.getLimit())`, causing the reactive pipeline to attempt to stream 2 billion entries and hold the connection open indefinitely, starving other clients from obtaining address book data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user omit the `fileId` field in `AddressBookQuery` so that `query.hasFileId()` returns false and `filter.fileId` is never set (remaining null), causing `AddressBookFilter.build()` to produce a filter with a null `fileId` that bypasses the `@NotNull` constraint at the controller layer and propagates a NullPointerException or ConstraintViolationException deep into the reactive pipeline, causing unhandled errors that crash the stream handler?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `setOnCancelHandler(disposable::dispose)` is only registered when `responseObserver instanceof ServerCallStreamObserver`, meaning if the observer is not of that type the disposable is never cancelled on client disconnect, causing leaked reactive subscriptions that accumulate over time and exhaust JVM heap memory, eventually crashing the gRPC service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user rapidly open and immediately cancel `getNodes()` streams in a loop, exploiting a race condition between the `disposable::dispose` cancel handler registration (which occurs after `subscribe()` returns) and the actual cancellation signal, leaving zombie subscriptions that hold database connections and scheduler threads, degrading service capacity to zero?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger processing of an `AddressBookEntry` with an extremely large `memo` field (up to the database column limit), causing `ByteString.copyFromUtf8(addressBookEntry.getMemo())` to allocate a large byte array per node per stream, and when multiplied across many concurrent `getNodes()` calls, exhaust JVM heap memory and trigger an OutOfMemoryError that shuts down the gRPC service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger processing of an `AddressBookEntry` with a very large `description` field, causing `nodeAddress.setDescription(addressBookEntry.getDescription())` to allocate large protobuf strings per response item, and when streamed across many concurrent connections, cause heap exhaustion and a total gRPC service crash?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger processing of an `AddressBookEntry` with a large `nodeCertHash` byte array, causing `ProtoUtil.toByteString(addressBookEntry.getNodeCertHash())` to allocate large `ByteString` objects per node per stream, and when multiplied across many concurrent `getNodes()` calls, exhaust JVM heap and crash the service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger processing of an `AddressBookEntry` with a large `publicKey` string, causing `nodeAddress.setRSAPubKey(addressBookEntry.getPublicKey())` to allocate large string objects per node per stream, and when multiplied across many concurrent `getNodes()` calls, exhaust JVM heap and crash the service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger processing of an `AddressBookEntry` with a very large number of `serviceEndpoints`, causing the `for (final var s : addressBookEntry.getServiceEndpoints())` loop to iterate over thousands of endpoints per node, and when multiplied across many concurrent `getNodes()` calls, cause CPU and memory exhaustion that shuts down the gRPC service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger processing of an `AddressBookEntry` whose `serviceEndpoint.getIpAddressV4()` contains a hostname string (not a pure IP), causing `InetAddress.getByName(ipAddress)` to perform a blocking DNS lookup on a reactive/non-blocking thread, blocking the `Schedulers.boundedElastic()` thread pool and causing all concurrent `getNodes()` streams to stall, effectively shutting down the gRPC service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger processing of an `AddressBookEntry` whose `ipAddressV4` field contains a slow-to-resolve hostname, causing `InetAddress.getByName(ipAddress)` to block for the full DNS timeout (up to 30 seconds) per entry, and when multiplied across many nodes and concurrent streams, exhaust all available threads and cause a total service outage?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger processing of an `AddressBookEntry` with a specially crafted `ipAddressV4` value that is not blank but causes `InetAddress.getByName()` to throw an `UnknownHostException`, and by doing so repeatedly across many concurrent streams, flood the log system with `log.warn()` calls that cause I/O saturation and degrade service performance to the point of a shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a malformed protobuf `AddressBookQuery` message with an oversized payload, causing protobuf deserialization in the gRPC framework to consume excessive CPU or memory before even reaching `getNodes()`, and by sending many such requests in parallel, cause a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate clients from querying the address book?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any per-client connection limit or IP-based throttling on the `getNodes()` gRPC endpoint to open the maximum number of HTTP/2 streams allowed by the server configuration, saturating the gRPC server's stream capacity and preventing any new connections from being established, causing a total network address book service outage?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Mono.fromCallable(() -> toFilter(request))` pattern, where an unchecked exception thrown inside the callable is wrapped by `onErrorMap(ProtoUtil::toStatusRuntimeException)` and sent to `responseObserver::onError`, but if `ProtoUtil::toStatusRuntimeException` itself throws, the error propagates uncaught and crashes the reactive pipeline, potentially destabilizing the gRPC server?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a continuous stream of `getNodes()` requests with valid `fileId` values (0.0.101 or 0.0.102) but `limit=0`, causing the `repeatWhen(RepeatSpec.create(c -> !context.isComplete(), Long.MAX_VALUE))` loop to run indefinitely (since `context.isComplete()` may never become true if the address book is empty), holding scheduler threads forever and causing a gradual service degradation leading to total shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getNodes()` performs no authentication or authorization check before invoking `networkService::getNodes`, allowing any unauthenticated client to trigger database queries (`addressBookRepository.findLatestTimestamp`, `nodeStakeRepository.findAllStakeByConsensusTimestamp`) on every request, and by flooding with requests, exhaust the database connection pool and prevent the mirror node from processing any further queries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send an `AddressBookQuery` with a `FileID` containing extreme shard/realm/num values (e.g., shard=Long.MAX_VALUE), causing `EntityId.of(query.getFileId())` to produce an `EntityId` that passes the `hasFileId()` check but fails validation deep in the service layer with an unhandled exception that propagates through the reactive pipeline and crashes the stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of backpressure in the `.subscribe(responseObserver::onNext, responseObserver::onError, responseObserver::onCompleted)` call, where `onNext` is called as fast as the reactive pipeline produces items without any demand signaling from the client, causing unbounded in-memory buffering of `NodeAddress` objects and eventual heap exhaustion that crashes the gRPC service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a `getNodes()` request and then hold the stream open without reading responses (slow client / zero-window attack), causing the gRPC server to buffer all `NodeAddress` responses in memory via `responseObserver::onNext`, and by doing this across many connections, exhaust JVM heap and crash the service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `flatMapMany(networkService::getNodes)` call which has no concurrency limit, allowing the reactive pipeline to spawn unbounded inner subscriptions if the upstream emits multiple items, potentially causing uncontrolled parallelism that exhausts thread and memory resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated `getNodes()` requests that each trigger `nodeStakeRepository.findAllStakeByConsensusTimestamp(nodeStakeTimestamp)` loading the entire node stake map into memory per request, and by flooding with concurrent requests, cause repeated large memory allocations that trigger GC pressure and eventually an OutOfMemoryError crashing the service?
